package_version_anchors.mace-torch: >=0.3.15 (conda-envs/mace-agent/core_env.yaml). NOT importable in miniconda base; the per-agent env (mace-agent) is not installed on this machine either, so MACE units/API are anchored to AtomisticSkills wrapper usage + canonical mace docstrings, not a read package source line.
package_version_anchors.matgl: 4.x (conda-envs/matgl-agent/core_env.yaml pins 'matgl'; wrapper comment 'Canonical pretrained model names in matgl 4.x', matgl_wrapper.py:38). NOT importable in base; anchored to wrapper usage + matgl docstrings.
package_version_anchors.fairchem-core: >=2.18.0 (conda-envs/fairchem-agent/core_env.yaml). NOT importable in base; anchored to wrapper usage + fairchem-core v2 API (fairchem.core.FAIRChemCalculator, pretrained_mlip, load_predict_unit).
package_version_anchors.matcalc: the shared driver (ElasticityCalc, EOSCalc, PhononCalc, RelaxCalc, PropCalc/MDCalc). The mat-* physics skills never touch a raw calculator; they call wrapper.create_calculator() and hand the ASE calculator to a matcalc PropCalc. NOT importable in base.

scan.role_in_software: An MLIP (MACE via mace-torch, matgl M3GNet/CHGNet/TensorNet, fairchem-core UMA/ESEN) is a REPRESENTATION of the opaque Potential node: a parameterized Born-Oppenheimer PES. Each wrapper (src/utils/mlips/{mace,matgl,fairchem}/*_wrapper.py) subclasses MLIPModel (base.py) and exposes ONE physics-relevant method, create_calculator() -> ase.calculators.calculator.Calculator. From that single ASE calculator the whole map is fed: TotalEnergy (atoms.get_potential_energy, eV), Forces (atoms.get_forces, eV/A), Stress (atoms.get_stress, eV/A^3 Voigt-6). matcalc drivers then post-process the SAME calculator into ElasticConstants, BulkModulus, ShearModulus, the Birch-Murnaghan EOS, phonon force constants, and MD trajectories. So on the map an MLIP is the SOURCE realization of Potential -> {TotalEnergy, Forces, Stress}; everything mat-* pymatgen/matcalc computes downstream (already catalogued in pymatgen-atomistic-skills.json) consumes these three.
scan.provenance_scheme_recommended: Instances on the map that today credit only the SKILL (docs/data/codes.json 'mat-elasticity', 'mat-diffusion-analysis') should also credit the MODEL as a representation of Potential. The model identity is a 3-field tuple carried on the Potential representation: (a) model_type in {mace, matgl, fairchem} (the --model_type CLI arg / load_wrapper first arg, loader.py:23-44); (b) model_name (the canonical checkpoint id after alias resolution, e.g. MACE-MH-1 / M3GNet-PES-MatPES-PBE-2025.2 / uma-s-1p2); (c) prediction head/task (MACE head 'omat_pbe', matgl none, fairchem task_name 'omat'/'omol'/'oc22'). These three are exactly what base.py:719-745 already serializes into md_inputs.json as {model_name, prediction_head}. A fourth field, is_fine_tuned (base.py:321, matgl_wrapper.py:265, fairchem_wrapper.py:201), distinguishes a foundation checkpoint from a fine-tuned one. Recommended: add a code key per model_type to codes.json (mace/matgl/fairchem) mapping Potential/TotalEnergy/Forces/Stress to the wrapper API with unit=eV / eV_per_A / eV_per_A3, and record (model_name, head, is_fine_tuned) as labels on the Potential-representation node so two instances from different checkpoints do not false-merge.
scan.canonical_units_note: The map's canonical units (omai/operator/registry.py QUANTITY_TAGS + QE/LAMMPS/pymatgen scans) are the reference. The MLIP calculators are ASE-native, and AtomisticSkills standardizes on ASE energy units project-wide (base.py:643-644 explicit comment 'ASE units: stress is in eV/A^3 ... We standardize to eV/A^3 across the project'). Unlike the pymatgen elasticity path (which stores eV/A^3 then *160.2176634 to GPa), the raw calculator E/F/S are in ASE units eV, eV/A, eV/A^3.

entries.name: mlip-potential-representation
entries.quantity: The parameterized Born-Oppenheimer potential-energy surface itself: an ASE Calculator wrapping a trained MLIP, the map's Potential node realized by a model checkpoint

dimension.note: operator / representation, not a single physical quantity; its outputs carry the dimensions (energy, force, stress)

entries.units: n/a (the checkpoint); its outputs are eV / eV/A / eV/A^3
entries.producing_api: src.utils.mlips.loader.load_wrapper(model_type, model_name, device) -> MLIPModel; wrapper.create_calculator() -> ase Calculator. MACE: mace_mp/MACECalculator/mace_off/mace_omol/mace_anicc (mace_wrapper.py:144-242). matgl: matgl.ext.ase.PESCalculator(potential=self.model, stress_unit='eV/A3') (matgl_wrapper.py:123-130). fairchem: fairchem.core.FAIRChemCalculator(predict_unit=self.model, task_name=target_task) (fairchem_wrapper.py:222-255).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: Potential is opaque (Phase-1). The MLIP is a representation of it; no unit. Model provenance is the discriminant (see provenance_scheme_recommended).

mace.default_checkpoint_wrapper: MACE -> MACE-MH-1 (multi-head foundation), head defaults to 'omat_pbe' (mace_wrapper.py:19-21, :203-212). Aliases MACE/MACE-MP also map to MACE-MH-1.
mace.default_checkpoint_mcp_tool: MACE-OMAT-0-small (src/mcp_server/mace_server.py:40,56). NOTE: the MCP tool default and the wrapper class default differ.
mace.checkpoint_families: MACE-MH-{0,1} (multi-head), MACE-MP-{small,medium,large}[-0b/-0b2/-0b3] (Materials Project), MACE-MPA-0, MACE-OMAT-0-{small,medium} (OMat24), MACE-MATPES-{PBE,R2SCAN}-0, MACE-OFF23-{small,medium,large} (organic), MACE-OMOL-extra-large (charge/spin), MACE-ANI-CC (mace_wrapper.py:17-50).
mace.head: multi-head MACE selects a training-data head; default 'omat_pbe' for MH models (mace_wrapper.py:203-212). Head is a required provenance field.

matgl.checkpoint_families: M3GNet-PES-MatPES-{PBE,r2SCAN}-2025.2, CHGNet-PES-MatPES-{PBE,r2SCAN}-2025.2.10, TensorNet-PES-*, QET-PES-*, SO3Net-PES-ANI-1x, plus MEGNet/M3GNet PROPERTY models (BandGap, Eform) that are NOT PES models (matgl_wrapper.py:39-81).
matgl.head: none (single-task PES). Functional (PBE vs r2SCAN) is encoded in the checkpoint name.

fairchem.default_checkpoint_wrapper: class default model_name 'EquiformerV2' (fairchem_wrapper.py:116) but real usage resolves a UMA/ESEN key; default TASK is 'omat' for non-esen, 'omol' for esen (fairchem_wrapper.py:237-241).
fairchem.checkpoint_families: uma-{s,m}-1p{1,2} and uma-s-1 (Universal, tasks omat/omol/oc22), esen-{md,sm}-{direct,conserving}-all-{omol,oc25} (molecular / catalysis) (fairchem_wrapper.py:23-77).
fairchem.head: task_name in {omat, omol, oc22} selected at calculator build; omat is the materials/solid task. Task is a required provenance field (it changes the reference energies and which of charge/spin are read).

usage: mat-sample-pes-by-md/scripts/run_sampling.py:145,147,153,155 (MatGLWrapper + MACEWrapper instantiated directly); sampler.py:322-323,378 (RelaxCalc + NPT MD atoms.calc)

MCP-tool skills (relax_structure/run_md/predict_features): mat-surface-energy/SKILL.md:22-23,86-88 (matgl CHGNet r2SCAN); mat-stability/SKILL.md:48-51,104-107 (matgl TensorNet r2SCAN); mat-defect-energy/SKILL.md:32-33,94-95 (mace); mat-melting-point/SKILL.md:27,99 (mace run_md); mat-diffusion-analysis/SKILL.md:15-20 (run_md)
mat-lammps-md/SKILL.md:3,10,41-71 (does NOT use the Python wrappers; runs LAMMPS binaries with MACE / MatGL-CHGNet / FairChem pair styles)
mat-elemental-energies: does NOT invoke an MLIP (reads a precomputed eV/atom reference library, SKILL.md:23-31)
source: src/utils/mlips/base.py:25-160 (MLIPModel ABC; create_calculator abstract; get_model_info serializes model_name/version/is_fine_tuned/supports_charge_spin)

maps_to.notes: The MLIP is a REPRESENTATION of the opaque Phase-1 Potential node (registry: 'Born-Oppenheimer potential of the material'), exactly as ASE's 'Potential' code entry in codes.json (ase.Atoms.calc) is. Recommend: give mace/matgl/fairchem their own codes.json entries for Potential (api = wrapper.create_calculator()) plus TotalEnergy/Forces/Stress, and carry the (model_name, head/task, is_fine_tuned) provenance tuple as labels so distinct checkpoints stay distinct. This is the parent of the E/F/S entries below and the thing mat-elasticity/-diffusion instances should credit alongside the skill.

entries.name: mlip-total-energy
entries.quantity: MLIP-predicted potential energy of the configuration (the model's estimate of the DFT total energy the checkpoint was trained on)
entries.units: eV per CONFIGURATION (per simulation cell), via ase.Atoms.get_potential_energy(). Per-atom (eV/atom) is the derived form used in stability/benchmark flows (energy / num_atoms).
entries.producing_api: atoms.calc = wrapper.create_calculator(); atoms.get_potential_energy() (base.py:635). matcalc MD path also exposes potential_energy averaged over frames (md_runner.py:400,404-409).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV matches the map's TotalEnergy canonical (registry total_energy 'per simulation cell'). No conversion. QE grounds the same node in Ry (1 Ry = 13.605693 eV); the MLIP is trained to reproduce a specific DFT functional's total energy, so the reference/zero differs by functional (PBE vs r2SCAN vs OMat) AND by the model's per-element reference energies (fairchem atom_refs, fairchem_wrapper.py:652-673). Cross-model energies are NOT directly comparable in absolute value; only relative energies / per-atom differences are.

usage: src/utils/mlips/base.py:401-411 (relax: energy_val = float(atoms.get_potential_energy()); written to relaxed_energy.txt for compute_ehull.py)
ml-mlip-benchmark/scripts/run_benchmark.py:116-123 (pred_e = float(pred['energy'])/num_atoms; standardized to energy PER ATOM), :176 ('Energy Metrics (eV/atom)')

maps_to.notes: Direct match, eV per cell. Same per-cell-vs-per-atom trap flagged in the pymatgen scan: the raw calculator returns per-cell eV, but benchmark (run_benchmark.py:119) and stability flows use eV/atom. PROVENANCE CAVEAT: the absolute energy zero is model+functional specific (per-element reference energies), so an EXPECTED_AGREE across two model_names or two functionals must be on RELATIVE energies, not absolute. This is the node mat-stability/-defect/-voltage/-surface energy differences (pymatgen scan) are built on.

entries.name: mlip-forces
entries.quantity: Per-atom forces = negative gradient of the MLIP energy w.r.t. nuclear positions
entries.producing_api: atoms.get_forces() (base.py:636); consumed by ASE optimizers (FIRE/BFGS/LBFGS via FrechetCellFilter, base.py:376-391) for relaxation and by matcalc PhononCalc for finite-difference force sets.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV/A. The map's forces tag is 'Per-atom Hellmann-Feynman forces'. QE grounds Forces in Ry/bohr (1 Ry/bohr = 25.71104 eV/A). For a conservative MLIP F = -dE/dr exactly (autograd); for a DIRECT-force model (fairchem 'esen-*-direct', fairchem_wrapper.py:51,61,72) forces are a separate model head and are NOT the exact gradient of the energy head, so energy-conservation is not guaranteed (an MD gauge concern). Record force_type in {conservative, direct} as a scheme.

usage: src/utils/mlips/base.py:389-391 (ASE optimizer runs to fmax in eV/A; default fmax 0.01 base.py:287)

maps_to.notes: Direct match, eV/A. New provenance flag needed: force_type conservative vs direct. UMA and MACE and matgl PES models are conservative (forces = autograd of energy); fairchem 'esen-*-direct' models predict forces from a separate head (fairchem_wrapper.py:51,61 domain='molecular'/'catalysis'). The 'conserving' vs 'direct' distinction is in the fairchem checkpoint name itself. Not a unit trap, a physics/gauge trap for MD.

entries.name: mlip-stress
entries.quantity: Cell-averaged macroscopic stress from the MLIP (virial), consumed by the elastic-tensor and EOS fits and by NPT barostats
index_structure: Voigt-6: xx, yy, zz, yz, xz, xy (ASE Voigt order)
entries.units: eV/ANGSTROM^3 (eV/A^3), Voigt 6-vector, via ase.Atoms.get_stress(). AtomisticSkills standardizes stress to eV/A^3 project-wide (base.py:643-644 explicit comment). matgl PESCalculator is constructed with stress_unit='eV/A3' to FORCE this (matgl_wrapper.py:129) because matgl's native stress default is GPa - a real trap avoided in-code.
entries.producing_api: atoms.get_stress() (base.py:642); matgl PESCalculator(stress_unit='eV/A3') (matgl_wrapper.py:128-130); fed to matcalc ElasticityCalc/EOSCalc (calculate_elasticity.py:68, calculate_eos.py:62). NPT barostat consumes it after pressure is converted bar->eV/A^3 via units.bar (base.py:788, md_runner.py:186).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: map's Stress canonical is a pressure tensor (registry: 'pressure convention'). 1 eV/A^3 = 160.2176634 GPa = 1602.1766208 kbar (EXACT: the benchmark uses -1/1602.1766208 to convert VASP kbar targets to eV/A^3, run_benchmark.py:53-55, sign flip because VASP stress = -sigma). ASE Voigt ORDER is [xx, yy, zz, yz, xz, yz->xy] i.e. (0,1,2)=diagonal, (3,4,5)=(yz,xz,xy) - DIFFERENT from LAMMPS compute stress/atom order (xx,yy,zz,xy,xz,yz) and from pymatgen tensor.voigt reverse map. SIGN: ASE get_stress returns sigma with the physics sign (dU/deps / V), positive-under-tension; VASP kbar is opposite sign (hence the -1). matcalc's elastic fit on the MLIP path does NOT take pymatgen's from_independent_strains(vasp=True) -0.1 branch, so no kbar/sign correction is applied (consistent with eV/A^3 input).

usage: src/utils/mlips/base.py:642-649 (stress = atoms.get_stress(); comment 'ASE units: stress is in eV/A^3 ... standardize to eV/A^3')
ml-mlip-benchmark/scripts/run_benchmark.py:53-55 (--vasp-stress-conversion: target stress *= -1/1602.1766208 to go kbar -> eV/A^3)
mat-elasticity/scripts/calculate_elasticity.py:61,68 (calculator -> ElasticityCalc -> internally fits eV/A^3 stresses; skill then *160.2176634 to GPa at :86-88)
source: src/utils/mlips/base.py:787-788 (pressure * units.bar -> eV/A^3 for MatCalc NPT)

maps_to.notes: Direct match. TWO traps: (1) UNIT+SIGN: eV/A^3 (positive-under-tension) vs VASP kbar (-1/1602.1766208 = -0.000624151, i.e. kbar->eV/A^3 WITH sign flip; run_benchmark.py:53-55). matgl's native GPa default is overridden in-code to eV/A^3 (matgl_wrapper.py:129) - a consumer bypassing the wrapper would get GPa. (2) ORDER: ASE Voigt (xx,yy,zz,yz,xz,xy) differs from LAMMPS (xx,yy,zz,xy,xz,yz). This is the Stress that feeds the ElasticConstants/BulkModulus already catalogued in the pymatgen scan; the *160.2176634 there converts the ElasticTensor, and this eV/A^3 stress is its raw input.

entries.name: chgnet-magmoms
entries.quantity: Per-site magnetic moments predicted by CHGNet (its distinguishing extra output beyond E/F/S)

dimension.note: Bohr magnetons mu_B per site (CHGNet convention)

entries.units: mu_B per site (CHGNet magmom head). NOT consumed from the LIVE MLIP calculator anywhere in AtomisticSkills.
entries.producing_api: CHGNet's calculator exposes magmoms; in the matgl 4.x CHGNet-PES wrapper the capability flag get_model_capabilities()['charges'] is True for CHGNet (matgl_wrapper.py:288). The mat-sample-pes-by-md custom calculators list 'magmoms' as an implemented property with a calc_magmom flag (feature_calculators.py:46,56,263,447) but default it False and no analysis reads it. The one skill that parses magnetic moments (mat-magnetic-density/scripts/parse_magnetic_moments.py:70-77) reads them from SERIALIZED VASP/MCP output (calc_data['magmom'] / output['magnetic_moments']), NOT from a live CHGNet run. So CHGNet's magmom head is never consumed as an MLIP output.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node; mu_B. Would map to the proposed magnetic_moment node (see pymatgen scan magnetic-moment entry).

source: CHGNet magmom head is a model-internal output (anchored to matgl/CHGNet docstrings; matgl not importable in base). No AtomisticSkills consumer.

maps_to.notes: CHGNet's signature capability (per-site magmoms, and it is a Charge-informed model) is PRESENT in the checkpoint but UNUSED by AtomisticSkills: no script reads calc.results magmoms. Record as an unclaimed model output. If the magnetism domain is ingested, CHGNet is a second source for magnetic_moment (mu_B) alongside VASP/MP. The matgl wrapper even mislabels it as 'charges' (matgl_wrapper.py:288) though CHGNet's extra head is magnetic moments; a reviewer should confirm the exact head semantics against CHGNet docs.

entries.name: committee-uncertainty
entries.quantity: Ensemble (committee) prediction uncertainty: standard deviation of energy and forces across N independently trained MACE checkpoints

dimension.note: sigma_E: energy (meV/atom reported); sigma_F: force (meV/A reported)

entries.units: energy_var and forces_var in (eV)^2 and (eV/A)^2 from MACE calc.results; converted to std in meV/atom and meV/A (sqrt(var)*1000/n_atoms for energy; sqrt(var)*1000 for force). run_committee_inference.py:119-125.
entries.producing_api: mace.calculators.MACECalculator(model_paths=[ckpt1..ckptN]) auto-runs each model and populates calc.results['energy_var'] and calc.results['forces_var'] after get_potential_energy() (run_committee_inference.py:79-106,118-120). Committee is MACE-specific (multiple checkpoints of the same architecture); matgl/fairchem wrappers have no committee path.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node. Variance across an ensemble is a REPRESENTATION-quality diagnostic on the Potential/E/F, not a physical observable. meV/atom (energy), meV/A (force).

source: MACE MACECalculator committee-variance behavior (anchored to usage + mace docstring; not in base env)

maps_to.notes: No uncertainty node on the map. Candidate diagnostic node(s): committee sigma over the Potential's TotalEnergy (meV/atom) and Forces (meV/A). It is a property OF the representation (an ensemble of checkpoints), attaching to the Potential-representation provenance rather than a new physical Space. Distinct from the model MAE metrics (mlip-benchmark-metrics below). MACE-only in AtomisticSkills.

entries.name: mlip-benchmark-metrics
entries.quantity: Accuracy of an MLIP against a labeled reference (DFT) dataset: MAE/RMSE of energy, forces, stress

dimension.note: E: eV/atom (reported meV/atom); F: eV/A (reported meV/A); S: eV/A^3

entries.units: energy MAE/RMSE in eV/atom natively (run_benchmark.py:176), plotted as meV/atom (*1000, plot_benchmark.py:99-100); force MAE/RMSE eV/A -> meV/A (plot_benchmark.py:114-115,120); stress in eV/A^3 (after the kbar conversion).
entries.producing_api: ml-mlip-benchmark/scripts/run_benchmark.py evaluate_metrics(preds, targets) = {mae, rmse} over a dataset; predictions come from wrapper.static_calculation batch; energy standardized per-atom (pred_e/num_atoms).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node. A model-quality metric against DFT labels, not a physical observable. eV/atom, eV/A, eV/A^3.

usage: ml-mlip-benchmark/scripts/run_benchmark.py:19-26 (evaluate_metrics: mae, rmse), :116-128 (per-atom energy, forces), :53-55 (stress unit conversion)
source: src/utils/mlips/base.py:576-620 (static_calculation batch produces the predictions benchmarked)

maps_to.notes: Model-validation metric (MLIP vs DFT reference), a diagnostic ON the Potential representation, not a physics-DAG quantity. Belongs to representation-quality provenance. Records the eV/atom / eV/A / eV/A^3 native units and the meV reporting convention. Confirms the E/F/S unit findings independently (energy per-atom, forces eV/A, stress eV/A^3 with the -1/1602.1766208 kbar conversion).

entries.name: atomic-features-descriptors
entries.quantity: Per-atom latent descriptors (learned invariant features) extracted from an MLIP for downstream ML (structure novelty, property prediction)

dimension.note: dimensionless learned feature vector, shape (n_atoms, feature_dim)

entries.units: dimensionless (model-internal embedding). MACE: calc.get_descriptors(atoms, invariants_only=True) (mace_wrapper.py:259). matgl: inner model feature_dict node_feat (matgl_wrapper.py:196-233). fairchem: not implemented (placeholder, fairchem_wrapper.py:606-609).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no map node; dimensionless learned representation, not a physical quantity.

maps_to.notes: Learned descriptors are an internal representation, not a physics observable. Out of the physics-DAG scope (analogous to Kohn-Sham wavefunctions being hidden). Consumed by ml-property-predict / structure-novelty ML, not by the thermal/mechanical map. fairchem has no descriptor API.

encode_candidates_ranked: 1. Potential-representation provenance tuple (model_type, model_name, head/task, is_fine_tuned) on the existing Potential node. HIGHEST value: it is the fix for the paper's stated gap (mat-elasticity instances credit only the skill; they should credit the MODEL). Already serialized by base.py:719-745. Encode as codes.json entries mace/matgl/fairchem + labels on Potential.

2. mlip-total-energy / mlip-forces / mlip-stress: already-mapped to TotalEnergy/Forces/Stress. Encode the eV / eV/A / eV/A^3 units on the mace/matgl/fairchem code entries (mirroring the ase Potential entry in codes.json) and the force_type {conservative, direct} + stress order/sign schemes.
3. committee-uncertainty (energy_uncertainty/force_uncertainty, meV/atom, meV/A): a representation-quality node attached to Potential provenance. MACE-only.
4. chgnet-magmoms (magnetic_moment, mu_B): an UNCLAIMED CHGNet output; only if the magnetism domain is ingested. Low priority (no AtomisticSkills consumer today).
5. atomic-features-descriptors and mlip-benchmark-metrics: representation-only diagnostics; do not encode as physics nodes.
unit_traps: STRESS eV/A^3 vs GPa vs kbar. The raw MLIP stress is eV/A^3 (ASE get_stress), standardized project-wide (base.py:643-644). matgl's PESCalculator NATIVE stress default is GPa; the wrapper overrides it with stress_unit='eV/A3' (matgl_wrapper.py:129) - a consumer that builds PESCalculator without that kwarg gets GPa (160.22x). 1 eV/A^3 = 160.2176634 GPa = 1602.1766208 kbar. Benchmark converts VASP kbar targets with -1/1602.1766208 (SIGN FLIP included; run_benchmark.py:53-55).

STRESS SIGN. ASE get_stress uses positive-under-tension (dU/deps/V); VASP kbar is opposite sign. The -1 in the benchmark conversion is the sign, not a unit. matcalc's MLIP elastic path does NOT apply pymatgen's from_independent_strains(vasp=True) -0.1 kbar/sign correction (input is already signed eV/A^3).

STRESS VOIGT ORDER. ASE Voigt is (xx, yy, zz, yz, xz, xy); LAMMPS compute stress/atom is (xx, yy, zz, xy, xz, yz); pymatgen Tensor.voigt uses reverse_voigt_map [[0,5,4],[5,1,3],[4,3,2]]. Three different packings for the same tensor.

ENERGY per-cell vs per-atom vs per-element-reference. Raw get_potential_energy is eV/cell; benchmark/stability use eV/atom (/num_atoms). Absolute energy zero is model+functional specific (fairchem atom_refs, per-element; PBE vs r2SCAN vs OMat). Cross-model/-functional agreement must be on RELATIVE energies only.

FORCES conservative vs direct. MACE, matgl PES, UMA are conservative (autograd of energy). fairchem 'esen-*-direct' checkpoints predict forces from a separate head (fairchem_wrapper.py:51,61) - not exactly -dE/dr, so energy is not conserved in NVE. A physics gauge, not a unit trap.

DEFAULT MODEL DIFFERS wrapper vs MCP tool. Wrapper class defaults (MACE->MACE-MH-1 head omat_pbe; matgl M3GNet->M3GNet-PES-MatPES-PBE-2025.2) differ from the MCP tool defaults (mace_server MACE-OMAT-0-small; fairchem_server uma-s-1p2; matgl_server CHGNet-PES-MatPES-PBE-2025.2.10). An instance's model_name must be read from the actual call, not assumed from a default.

MACE HEAD. Multi-head MACE (MH-0/MH-1) requires a head; default omat_pbe (mace_wrapper.py:203-212). The head selects the training-data domain and therefore the functional/reference - a required provenance field, not cosmetic.
MD PRESSURE unit. NPT target pressure is passed in bar and converted to eV/A^3 via units.bar (base.py:788); the barostat consumes eV/A^3 stress.
open_questions: None of mace-torch, matgl, or fairchem-core is importable in the miniconda base env, and the per-agent conda envs (mace-agent/matgl-agent/fairchem-agent) are not installed on this machine. All units (eV, eV/A, eV/A^3), the ASE Voigt stress order, the MACE committee calc.results keys (energy_var/forces_var), and the CHGNet magmom head are anchored to AtomisticSkills wrapper usage + canonical package docstrings, NOT to a read package source line. A reviewer with an agent env installed should confirm: (a) ase get_stress Voigt order and sign; (b) matgl PESCalculator's native (non-eV/A3) stress default really is GPa; (c) MACECalculator populates energy_var/forces_var only with >=2 model_paths.

PROVENANCE SCHEME: confirm the map wants the (model_type, model_name, head/task, is_fine_tuned) tuple as LABELS on the Potential-representation node (so distinct checkpoints don't false-merge), vs. as separate code entries. The paper's mat-elasticity instance credits only the skill today; the encode stage must decide where the model credit lives (a Potential-representation label is recommended).

CHGNet MAGMOMS: the matgl wrapper flags CHGNet with capability key 'charges' (matgl_wrapper.py:288), but CHGNet's documented extra head is per-site MAGNETIC MOMENTS (mu_B). Confirm the exact head semantics (magmoms vs charges) against CHGNet/matgl 4.x docs before mapping to magnetic_moment. No AtomisticSkills script consumes either, so this is an unclaimed output.

FORCE_TYPE scheme: fairchem ships both 'conserving' and 'direct' checkpoints (checkpoint name encodes it). Decide whether force_type {conservative, direct} is a scheme on the Potential representation (it affects energy conservation in MD, i.e. the Trajectory gauge and any Green-Kubo/HeatCurrent built from it).

matcalc as the true producer: ElasticityCalc/EOSCalc/PhononCalc/MDCalc wrap the ASE calculator and do the fitting/finite-difference; the wrapper only supplies create_calculator(). The already-mapped ElasticConstants/BulkModulus/EOS/phonon nodes (pymatgen scan) are produced by matcalc-on-MLIP, so the map's Potential->{those} edges should record BOTH the matcalc driver AND the MLIP checkpoint in provenance.

MLIP -> LAMMPS export: mat-lammps-md ships per-model examples (examples/{mace,matgl,fairchem}/*.sh) that compile the checkpoint into a LAMMPS pair_style (e.g. MACE *-lammps.pt, conda-envs/mace-agent/README). This is a SECOND realization of the same Potential (as an ASE calculator vs a LAMMPS pair_style); the LAMMPS-scan Potential node (codes.json lammps 'Potential': pair_style) and this MLIP node are the same physical PES via two engines - a cross-engine EXPECTED_AGREE candidate.
